comment: This is generated by ESQL's MvLikeTests. Do not edit it. See docs/reference/query-languages/esql/README.md for how to regenerate it.
name: mv_like
description:
Returns `true` when any value yielded by `field` matches `pattern`, using the same wildcard syntax as `LIKE`.
`LIKE` is a single-value scalar: applied to a multivalue field it emits a warning and returns `null`, so this
is how you match a wildcard pattern against a multivalue field. A null or empty `field` returns `false`, and
because the result is never null the predicate composes under `AND`/`OR`/`NOT`.

params.name: field
params.description: Multivalue expression to test. If null or empty, the function returns `false`.

params.name: pattern
params.description: Wildcard pattern. Must be a constant. `*` matches any run of characters, `?` matches a single character; escape either with `\`.

params.name: field
params.description: Multivalue expression to test. If null or empty, the function returns `false`.

params.name: pattern
params.description: Wildcard pattern. Must be a constant. `*` matches any run of characters, `?` matches a single character; escape either with `\`.

params.name: field
params.description: Multivalue expression to test. If null or empty, the function returns `false`.

params.name: pattern
params.description: Wildcard pattern. Must be a constant. `*` matches any run of characters, `?` matches a single character; escape either with `\`.

params.name: field
params.description: Multivalue expression to test. If null or empty, the function returns `false`.

params.name: pattern
params.description: Wildcard pattern. Must be a constant. `*` matches any run of characters, `?` matches a single character; escape either with `\`.

examples:
ROW names = ["Anna", "Bob"]
| EVAL no_carl = NOT mv_like(names, "Carl*")
